 146→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 147→                ),
 148→            ),
 149→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 150→            setOf(
 151→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 152→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 153→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 154→            ),
 155→    ) {
 156→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 157→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 158→
 159→    private var line = 1
 160→
 161→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 162→
 163→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 164→
 165→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 166→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 167→        indentConfig =
 168→            IndentConfig(
 169→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 170→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 171→            )
 172→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 173→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 174→        }
 175→    }
 176→
 177→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 178→        node: ASTNode,
 179→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 180→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 181→    ) {
 182→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 183→            // File should not start with a whitespace
 184→            node
 185→                .nextLeaf()
 186→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 187→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 188→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 189→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 190→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 191→                    }
 192→                }
 193→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 194→        }
 195→
 196→        when {
 197→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 198→                line++
 199→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 200→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 201→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 202→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 203→                    )
 204→                }
 205→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 206→            }
 207→
 208→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 209→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 210→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 211→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 212→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 213→                startIndentContext(
 214→                    fromAstNode = node,
 215→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 216→                )
 217→
 218→            node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY -> {
 219→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isPartOfClassWithAMultilinePrimaryConstructor()) {
 220→                    // Contrary to the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter, indent the super type call entry so that it looks better in case it
 221→                    // is followed by another super type:
 222→                    //      class Foo(
 223→                    //          val bar1: Bar,
 224→                    //          val bar2: Bar,
 225→                    //      ) : FooBar(
 226→                    //              bar1,
 227→                    //              bar2
 228→                    //          ),
 229→                    //          BarFoo,
 230→                    startIndentContext(
 231→                        fromAstNode = node,
 232→                        childIndent = indentConfig.indent,
 233→                        activated = true,
 234→                    )
 235→                } else {
 236→                    startIndentContext(
 237→                        fromAstNode = node,
 238→                        lastChildIndent = "",
 239→                    )
 240→                }
 241→            }
 242→
 243→            node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
 244→                visitValueArgument(node)
 245→
 246→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 247→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 248→
 249→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
 250→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF &&
 251→                (codeStyle != ktlint_official || !node.isPartOf(IF)) ->
 252→                startIndentContext(node)
 253→
 254→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 255→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 256→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 257→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
 258→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
 259→                startIndentContext(node)
 260→
 261→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 262→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 263→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 264→                startIndentContext(
 265→                    fromAstNode = node,
 266→                    childIndent = "",
 267→                )
 268→
 269→            node.elementType == IF ->
 270→                visitIf(node)
 271→
 272→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 273→                visitLbrace(node)
 274→
 275→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 276→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 277→                startIndentContext(
 278→                    fromAstNode = node,
 279→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 280→                )
 281→
 282→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 283→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 284→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 285→
 286→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 287→                visitValueParameter(node)
 288→
 289→            node.elementType == FUN ->
 290→                visitFun(node)
 291→
 292→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 293→                visitClass(node)
 294→
 295→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 296→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 297→
 298→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 299→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 300→
 301→            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 302→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 303→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 304→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 305→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 306→                ) {
 307→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 308→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 309→                    //    .bar[0] {
 310→                    //        "foobar"
 311→                    //    }
 312→                    startIndentContext(
 313→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 314→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 315→                    )
 316→                } else if (node.prevCodeSibling().isElvisOperator()) {
 317→                    startIndentContext(node)
 318→                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
 319→                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
 320→                } else {
 321→                    startIndentContext(node)
 322→                }
 323→            }
 324→
 325→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 326→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 327→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 328→
 329→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 330→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 331→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 332→
 333→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 334→                visitWhen(node)
 335→
 336→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 337→                visitWhenEntry(node)
 338→
 339→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 340→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 341→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 342→
 343→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 344→                visitKdoc(node)
 345→
 346→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 347→                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 348→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 349→
 350→            node.elementType == FOR ||
 351→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 352→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 353→
 354→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 355→                visitLBracket(node)
 356→
 357→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 358→                visitNullableType(node)
 359→
 360→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 361→                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 362→
 363→            node.elementType == TRY ->
 364→                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 365→
 366→            else -> {
 367→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 368→            }
 369→        }
 370→    }
 371→
 372→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfClassWithAMultilinePrimaryConstructor() =
 373→        parent { it.elementType == CLASS }
 374→            ?.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 375→            ?.textContains('\n') == true
 376→
 377→    private fun visitValueArgument(node: ASTNode) {
 378→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 379→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 380→            //     val foo = foo(
 381→            //         parameterName =
 382→            //             "The quick brown fox "
 383→            //                .plus("jumps ")
 384→            //                .plus("over the lazy dog"),
 385→            //     )
 386→            startIndentContext(
 387→                fromAstNode = node,
 388→                lastChildIndent = "",
 389→            )
 390→        }
 391→    }
 392→
 393→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 394→        node
 395→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 396→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 397→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 398→                val nextToAstNode =
 399→                    startIndentContext(
 400→                        fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 401→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 402→
 403→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 404→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 405→                    startIndentContext(
 406→                        fromAstNode = node,
 407→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 408→                        childIndent = "",
 409→                    )
 410→                }
 411→            }
 412→    }
 413→
 414→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 415→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 416→        node
 417→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 418→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 419→                nextToAstNode =
 420→                    startIndentContext(
 421→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 422→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 423→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 424→            }
 425→
 426→        node
 427→            .findChildByType(THEN)
 428→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 429→            ?.nextLeaf()
 430→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 431→                nextToAstNode =
 432→                    startIndentContext(
 433→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 434→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 435→                        childIndent = "",
 436→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 437→            }
 438→        node
 439→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 440→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 441→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 442→                nextToAstNode =
 443→                    startIndentContext(
 444→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 445→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 446→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 447→            }
 448→        startIndentContext(
 449→            fromAstNode = node,
 450→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 451→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 452→        )
 453→    }
 454→
 455→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 456→        // Outer indent context
 457→        val rbrace =
 458→            requireNotNull(
 459→                node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 460→            ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 461→        startIndentContext(
 462→            fromAstNode = node,
 463→            toAstNode = rbrace,
 464→            firstChildIndent = "",
 465→            lastChildIndent = "",
 466→        )
 467→
 468→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 469→        node
 470→            .treeParent
 471→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 472→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 473→            ?.let { arrow ->
 474→                startIndentContext(
 475→                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 476→                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 477→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 478→                )
 479→                startIndentContext(
 480→                    fromAstNode = node,
 481→                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 482→                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 483→                )
 484→            }
 485→    }
 486→
 487→    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 488→        if (isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 489→            // val fieldExample =
 490→            //      LongNameClass {
 491→            //              paramA,
 492→            //              paramB,
 493→            //              paramC ->
 494→            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 495→            //      }
 496→            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 497→        } else {
 498→            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 499→            // val fieldExample =
 500→            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 501→            //                     paramB,
 502→            //                     paramC ->
 503→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 504→            //     }
 505→            // val fieldExample =
 506→            //     someFunction(
 507→            //         1,
 508→            //         2,
 509→            //     ) { paramA,
 510→            //         paramB,
 511→            //         paramC ->
 512→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 513→            //     }
 514→            this
 515→                .takeIf { it.isPartOf(CALL_EXPRESSION) }
 516→                ?.treeParent
 517→                ?.leaves(false)
 518→                ?.takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
 519→                ?.sumOf { it.textLength }
 520→                ?.plus(2) // need to add spaces to compensate for "{ "
 521→                ?.let { length -> " ".repeat(length) }
 522→                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 523→        }
 524→
 525→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 526→        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 527→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 528→            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 529→
 530→    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 531→        startIndentContext(
 532→            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 533→            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 534→            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 535→            childIndent = "",
 536→        )
 537→    }
 538→
 539→    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 540→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 541→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 542→        node
 543→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 544→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 545→                nextToAstNode =
 546→                    startIndentContext(
 547→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 548→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 549→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 550→            }
 551→
 552→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 553→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 554→            //     fun process(
 555→            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 556→            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 557→            //     ): List<Output>
 558→            node
 559→                .findChildByType(COLON)
 560→                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 561→                    nextToAstNode =
 562→                        startIndentContext(
 563→                            fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 564→                            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 565→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 566→                }
 567→        }
 568→
 569→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 570→        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 571→        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 572→            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 573→            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 574→        ) {
 575→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 576→                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 577→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 578→            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 579→        } else {
 580→            startIndentContext(
 581→                fromAstNode = node,
 582→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 583→                childIndent = "",
 584→            )
 585→        }
 586→    }
 587→
 588→    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 589→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 590→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 591→        val eqOrBlock =
 592→            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 593→                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 594→        eqOrBlock?.let {
 595→            nextToAstNode =
 596→                startIndentContext(
 597→                    fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 598→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 599→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 600→        }
 601→
 602→        node
 603→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 604→            ?.let { typeReference ->
 605→                nextToAstNode =
 606→                    startIndentContext(
 607→                        fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 608→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 609→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 610→            }
 611→
 612→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 613→        startIndentContext(
 614→            fromAstNode = node,
 615→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 616→            childIndent = "",
 617→        )
 618→    }
 619→
 620→    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 621→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 622→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 623→        node
 624→            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 625→            ?.let { where ->
 626→                val typeConstraintList =
 627→                    requireNotNull(
 628→                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 629→                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 630→                require(
 631→                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 632→                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 633→                nextToAstNode =
 634→                    startIndentContext(
 635→                        fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 636→                        toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 637→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 638→            }
 639→
 640→        val primaryConstructor = node.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 641→        val containsConstructorKeyword = primaryConstructor?.findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_KEYWORD) != null
 642→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && primaryConstructor != null && containsConstructorKeyword) {
 643→            // Contrary to the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter, ident both constructor and super type list as follows:
 644→            //     class Foo
 645→            //        @Bar1 @Bar2
 646→            //        constructor(
 647→            //            foo1: Foo1,
 648→            //            foo2: Foo2,
 649→            //        ) : Foobar1(
 650→            //                "foobar1",
 651→            //                "foobar2",
 652→            //            ),
 653→            //            FooBar2,
 654→            val superTypeList = node.findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 655→            nextToAstNode =
 656→                startIndentContext(
 657→                    fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 658→                    toAstNode =
 659→                        superTypeList
 660→                            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 661→                            ?: nextToAstNode,
 662→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 663→
 664→            superTypeList
 665→                ?.findChildByType(COMMA)
 666→                ?.let { comma ->
 667→                    // In case of a multiline primary constructor the first super type is merged with the closing parenthesis of the
 668→                    // constructor. The start of the super type list does not activate the indent because it is not preceded by a newline.
 669→                    // To fix this, the super type entries on the next line (e.g. after the comma) have to be double indented.
 670→                    // Allow:
 671→                    //     class Foo(
 672→                    //         val bar1: Bar,
 673→                    //         val bar2: Bar,
 674→                    //     ) : FooBar(
 675→                    //             bar1,
 676→                    //             bar2
 677→                    //         ),
 678→                    //         BarFoo1
 679→                    val prevCodeLeaf =
 680→                        startIndentContext(
 681→                            fromAstNode = comma,
 682→                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 683→                            childIndent = indentConfig.indent.repeat(2),
 684→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 685→                    startIndentContext(
 686→                        fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 687→                        toAstNode = prevCodeLeaf,
 688→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 689→                }
 690→        } else {
 691→            node
 692→                .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 693→                ?.let { superTypeList ->
 694→                    nextToAstNode =
 695→                        startIndentContext(
 696→                            fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 697→                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 698→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 699→                }
 700→        }
 701→
 702→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 703→        startIndentContext(
 704→            fromAstNode = node,
 705→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 706→            childIndent = "",
 707→        )
 708→    }
 709→
 710→    private fun visitObjectDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 711→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 712→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 713→        node
 714→            .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 715→            ?.let { superTypeList ->
 716→                nextToAstNode =
 717→                    startIndentContext(
 718→                        fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 719→                        toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 720→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 721→            }
 722→
 723→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 724→        startIndentContext(
 725→            fromAstNode = node,
 726→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 727→            childIndent = "",
 728→        )
 729→    }

 731→    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {
 732→        if (isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node)) {
 733→            // Complex binary expression are nested in such a way that the indent context of the condition
 734→            // wrapper is not the last node on the stack
 735→            val conditionIndentContext =
 736→                indentContextStack.last { it.fromASTNode.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION }
 737→            // Create new indent context for the remainder (operator and right-hand side) of the binary expression
 738→            startIndentContext(
 739→                fromAstNode = node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)!!,
 740→                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 741→                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 742→                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,
 743→            )
 744→            startIndentContext(node.firstChildNode)
 745→        } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION ||
 746→            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
 747→        ) {
 748→            startIndentContext(node)
 749→        }
 750→    }
 751→
 752→    private fun visitIdentifierInProperty(node: ASTNode) {
 753→        startIndentContext(
 754→            fromAstNode = node,
 755→            toAstNode = node.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 756→        )
 757→    }

 759→    private fun visitWhen(node: ASTNode) {
 760→        var nextToASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 761→        node
 762→            .findChildByType(LPAR)
 763→            ?.let { lpar ->
 764→                nextToASTNode =
 765→                    startIndentContext(
 766→                        fromAstNode = lpar,
 767→                        toAstNode = node.findChildByType(RPAR)!!,
 768→                        lastChildIndent = "",
 769→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 770→            }
 771→        startIndentContext(
 772→            fromAstNode = node,
 773→            toAstNode = nextToASTNode,
 774→        )
 775→    }

 777→    private fun visitWhenEntry(node: ASTNode) {
 778→        node
 779→            .findChildByType(ARROW)
 780→            ?.let { arrow ->
 781→                val outerIndent =
 782→                    if (arrow.nextLeaf()?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 783→                        ""
 784→                    } else {
 785→                        indentConfig.indent
 786→                    }
 787→                startIndentContext(
 788→                    fromAstNode = arrow.nextLeaf()!!,
 789→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 790→                    firstChildIndent = outerIndent,
 791→                    lastChildIndent = outerIndent,
 792→                )
 793→                startIndentContext(
 794→                    fromAstNode = node,
 795→                    toAstNode = arrow,
 796→                    childIndent = "",
 797→                )
 798→            }
 799→    }

 801→    private fun visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node: ASTNode) {

 803→            fromAstNode = node,
 804→            toAstNode = node.nextCodeSibling()?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()!!,
 805→            childIndent = TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT,
 806→        )
 807→    }

 809→    private fun visitKdoc(node: ASTNode) {
 810→        node
 811→            .findChildByType(KDOC_START)
 812→            ?.nextLeaf()
 813→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 814→                startIndentContext(
 815→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 816→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 817→                    childIndent = KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT,
 818→                )
 819→            }
 820→    }

 822→    private fun visitPropertyAccessor(node: ASTNode) {
 823→        var nextToASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 824→        node
 825→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 826→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 827→                nextToASTNode =
 828→                    startIndentContext(
 829→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 830→                        toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 831→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 832→            }

 835→            fromAstNode = node,

 841→    private fun visitConditionalLoop(node: ASTNode) {
 842→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 843→        node
 844→            .findChildByType(BODY)
 845→            ?.takeIf { it.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType != LBRACE }
 846→            ?.let { rpar ->
 847→                startIndentContext(
 848→                    fromAstNode = rpar,
 849→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 850→                )
 851→            }
 852→        node
 853→            .findChildByType(CONDITION)
 854→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 855→                startIndentContext(fromAstNode)
 856→            }
 857→    }

 859→    private fun visitLBracket(node: ASTNode) {
 860→        node
 861→            .treeParent
 862→            .takeUnless {
 863→                // Should be resolved in IntelliJ IDEA default formatter:
 864→                // https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-14859/Too-little-indentation-inside-the-brackets-in-multiple-annotations-with-the-same-target
 865→                it.elementType == ANNOTATION
 866→            }?.findChildByType(RBRACKET)
 867→            ?.let { rbracket ->
 868→                startIndentContext(
 869→                    fromAstNode = node,
 870→                    toAstNode = rbracket,
 871→                    firstChildIndent = "",
 872→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 873→                )
 874→            }

 877→    private fun visitNullableType(node: ASTNode) {
 878→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 879→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 880→        node
 881→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 882→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 883→                nextToAstNode =
 884→                    startIndentContext(
 885→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 886→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 887→                        childIndent = "",
 888→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 889→            }

 896→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 897→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 898→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 899→        node
 900→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 901→            ?.let { eq ->
 902→                nextToAstNode =
 903→                    startIndentContext(
 904→                        fromAstNode = eq,
 905→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 906→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 907→            }
 908→
 909→        node
 910→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 911→            ?.let {
 912→                startIndentContext(
 913→                    fromAstNode = node,
 914→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 915→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 916→                )
 917→            }
 918→    }
 919→
 920→    private fun visitTryCatchFinally(node: ASTNode) {
 921→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 922→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 923→        node
 924→            .findChildByType(FINALLY)
 925→            ?.let { finally ->
 926→                nextToAstNode =
 927→                    startIndentContext(
 928→                        fromAstNode = finally,
 929→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 930→                        childIndent = "",
 931→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 932→            }
 933→
 934→        node
 935→            .findChildByType(CATCH)
 936→            ?.let { catch ->
 937→                nextToAstNode =
 938→                    startIndentContext(
 939→                        fromAstNode = catch,
 940→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 941→                        childIndent = "",
 942→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 943→            }
 944→
 945→        startIndentContext(
 946→            fromAstNode = node,
 947→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 948→            lastChildIndent = "",
 949→        )
 950→    }
 951→
 952→    private fun ASTNode.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations(): ASTNode {
 953→        require(elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR || elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 954→        return findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
 955→            ?.let { modifierList ->
 956→                modifierList
 957→                    .children()
 958→                    .firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
 959→                    ?: modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
 960→            }
 961→            ?: children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 962→            ?: this
 963→    }
 964→
 965→    private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {
 966→        var fromAstNode: ASTNode? = this
 967→        while (fromAstNode?.prevLeaf() != null &&
 968→            (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
 969→        ) {
 970→            fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()
 971→        }
 972→        return fromAstNode!!
 973→    }
 974→
 975→    private fun isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node: ASTNode) =
 976→        node
 977→            .parents()
 978→            .takeWhile { it.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION || it.elementType == CONDITION }
 979→            .lastOrNull()
 980→            ?.elementType == CONDITION
 981→
 982→    private fun currentIndent() =
 983→        indentContextStack
 984→            .peekLast()
 985→            .indent()
 986→
 987→    private fun startIndentContext(
 988→        fromAstNode: ASTNode,
 989→        toAstNode: ASTNode = fromAstNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 990→        nodeIndent: String = currentIndent(),
 991→        childIndent: String = indentConfig.indent,
 992→        firstChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 993→        lastChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 994→        activated: Boolean = false,
 995→    ): IndentContext =
 996→        IndentContext(
 997→            fromASTNode = fromAstNode,
 998→            toASTNode = toAstNode,
 999→            nodeIndent = nodeIndent,
1000→            firstChildIndent = firstChildIndent,
1001→            childIndent = childIndent,
1002→            lastChildIndent = lastChildIndent,
1003→            activated = activated,
1004→        ).also { newIndentContext ->
1005→            LOGGER.trace {
1006→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.nodeIndent)
1007→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.childIndent)
1008→                "Create new indent context (same as parent) with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
1009→                    "${fromAstNode.elementType}: ${newIndentContext.nodes}"
1010→            }
1011→            indentContextStack.addLast(newIndentContext)
1012→        }
1013→
1014→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
1015→        node: ASTNode,
1016→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1017→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1018→    ) {
1019→        while (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.toASTNode == node) {
1020→            LOGGER.trace {
1021→                val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
1022→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
1023→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
1024→                "Remove indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for ${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: " +
1025→                    "${indentContext.nodes}"
1026→            }
1027→            indentContextStack
1028→                .removeLast()
1029→                .also {
1030→                    LOGGER.trace {
1031→                        val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
1032→                        val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
1033→                        val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
1034→                        "Last indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
1035→                            "${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: ${indentContext.nodes}"
1036→                    }
1037→                }
1038→        }
1039→    }
1040→
1041→    override fun afterLastNode() {
1042→        require(indentContextStack.isEmpty()) {
1043→            indentContextStack
1044→                .joinToString(prefix = "Stack should be empty:\n\t", separator = "\n\t") { it.toString() }
1045→        }
1046→    }
1047→
1048→    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
1049→        node: ASTNode,
1050→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1051→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1052→    ) {
1053→        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
1054→            return
1055→        }
1056→
1057→        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
1058→        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
1059→        val text = node.text
1060→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1061→        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
1062→            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
1063→                emit(
1064→                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1065→                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
1066→                    true,
1067→                )
1068→            } else {
1069→                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
1070→                // been emitted.
1071→            }
1072→            LOGGER.trace {
1073→                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
1074→                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
1075→            }
1076→            if (autoCorrect) {
1077→                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1078→                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
1079→                )
1080→            }
1081→        } else {
1082→            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1083→        }
1084→    }
1085→
1086→    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
1087→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1088→        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
1089→            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1090→            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
1091→        }
1092→
1093→        nextLeaf
1094→            ?.parent(strict = false) { it.psi is PsiComment }
1095→            ?.let { comment ->
1096→                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
1097→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1098→                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
1099→                }
1100→                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
1101→                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
1102→                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
1103→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1104→                    return true
1105→                }
1106→            }
1107→        return false
1108→    }
1109→
1110→    private fun ASTNode?.isStartOfRawStringLiteral(): Boolean = this != null && this.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE && this.text == "\"\"\""
1111→
1112→    private fun ASTNode.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded() =
1113→        LOGGER.trace { "No indentation change required for $elementType: ${textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
1114→
1115→    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {
1116→        val lastIndexContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
1117→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1118→        val adjustedChildIndent =
1119→            when {
1120→                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
1121→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
1122→                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
1123→
1124→                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
1125→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
1126→                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
1127→
1128→                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent
1129→            }
1130→        return lastIndexContext.nodeIndent + adjustedChildIndent
1131→    }
1132→
1133→    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
1134→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1135→        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
1136→            SPACE -> {
1137→                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
1138→                    emit(
1139→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1140→                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1141→                        true,
1142→                    )
1143→                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
1144→                } else {
1145→                    nodeIndent
1146→                }
1147→            }
1148→
1149→            TAB -> {
1150→                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1151→                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1152→                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1153→                    emit(
1154→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1155→                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1156→                        true,
1157→                    )
1158→                    indentConfig
1159→                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1160→                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1161→                } else {
1162→                    nodeIndent
1163→                }
1164→            }
1165→        }
1166→    }
1167→
1168→    private fun visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(
1169→        node: ASTNode,
1170→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1171→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1172→    ) {
1173→        if (!this::stringTemplateIndenter.isInitialized) {
1174→            stringTemplateIndenter = StringTemplateIndenter(codeStyle, indentConfig)
1175→        }
1176→        stringTemplateIndenter.visitClosingQuotes(currentIndent(), node.treeParent, autoCorrect, emit)
1177→    }
1178→
1179→    private fun ASTNode?.isElvisOperator() =
1180→        this != null &&
1181→            elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE &&
1182→            firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
1183→
1184→    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {
1185→        require(elementType == WHITE_SPACE)
1186→        val acceptableTrailingSpaces =
1187→            when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1188→                KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1189→                    // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1190→                    // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1191→                    KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1192→                }
1193→
1194→                TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1195→                    // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1196→                    //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1197→                    //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1198→                    TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1199→                }
1200→
1201→                else -> ""
1202→            }
1203→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1204→        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1205→            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1206→        } else {
1207→            ""
1208→        }
1209→    }
1210→
1211→    private fun startNoIndentZone(node: ASTNode) =
1212→        IndentContext(
1213→            fromASTNode = node,
1214→            nodeIndent = "",
1215→            firstChildIndent = "",
1216→            childIndent = "",
1217→            lastChildIndent = "",
1218→            activated = true,
1219→        )
1220→
1221→    private companion object {
1222→        const val KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT = " "
1223→        const val TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT = "      " // Length of keyword "where" plus separating space
1224→        val CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION = setOf(DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION, SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION)
1225→    }
1226→
1227→    private data class IndentContext(
1228→        /**
1229→         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1230→         */
1231→        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1232→        /**
1233→         * The node at which the indent context ends. If null, then the context ends at the last child leaf of the node
1234→         * on which the indent context starts.
1235→         */
1236→        val toASTNode: ASTNode = fromASTNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
1237→        /**
1238→         * Cumulative indentation for the node. Normally this should be equal to a multiple of the
1239→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1240→         */
1241→        val nodeIndent: String,
1242→        /**
1243→         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1244→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1245→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1246→         */
1247→        val firstChildIndent: String,
1248→        /**
1249→         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1250→         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1251→         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1252→         */
1253→        val childIndent: String,
1254→        /**
1255→         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1256→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1257→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1258→         */
1259→        val lastChildIndent: String,
1260→        /**
1261→         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1262→         */
1263→        val activated: Boolean = false,
1264→    ) {
1265→        val nodes: String
1266→            get() =
1267→                fromASTNode
1268→                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1269→                    ?.leaves()
1270→                    .orEmpty()
1271→                    .takeWhile {
1272→                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1273→                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1274→                    }
1275→                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1276→                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1277→
1278→        fun indent() =
1279→            if (activated) {
1280→                nodeIndent + childIndent
1281→            } else {
1282→                nodeIndent
1283→            }
1284→
1285→        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1286→    }
1287→}
1288→
1289→private fun ASTNode.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline() = text.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1290→
1291→private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1292→    val (prefix, suffix) =
1293→        if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1294→            Pair("[", "]")
1295→        } else {
1296→            Pair("", "")
1297→        }
1298→    return prefix
1299→        .plus(
1300→            this
1301→                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1302→                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1303→        ).plus(suffix)
1304→}
1305→
1306→private class StringTemplateIndenter(
1307→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
1308→    private val indentConfig: IndentConfig,
1309→) {
1310→    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1311→        expectedIndent: String,
1312→        node: ASTNode,
1313→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1314→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1315→    ) {
1316→        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1317→        node
1318→            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1319→            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1320→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
1321→            ?.let { _ ->
1322→                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1323→                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1324→                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1325→                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1326→                    emit(
1327→                        node.startOffset,
1328→                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1329→                        false,
1330→                    )
1331→                    return
1332→                }
1333→
1334→                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()
1335→                val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
1336→                val correctedExpectedIndent =
1337→                    if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock()) {
1338→                        // Allow:
1339→                        //   fun foo(): String {
1340→                        //       return """
1341→                        //           some text
1342→                        //           """.trimIndent
1343→                        //   }
1344→                        node
1345→                            .indent(false)
1346→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1347→                    } else if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression()) {
1348→                        // Allow:
1349→                        //   fun foo(
1350→                        //       bar: String
1351→                        //   ) = """
1352→                        //       $bar
1353→                        //       """.trimIndent
1354→                        node
1355→                            .indent(false)
1356→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1357→                    } else if (prevLeaf?.text == "\n") {
1358→                        // In case the opening quotes are placed at the start of the line, then the closing quotes
1359→                        // should have no indent as well.
1360→                        ""
1361→                    } else {
1362→                        expectedIndent
1363→                    }
1364→                node.children()
1365→                    .forEach {
1366→                        if (it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" &&
1367→                            (
1368→                                it.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() ||
1369→                                    it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() ||
1370→                                    it.isClosingQuote()
1371→                                )
1372→                        ) {
1373→                            val (actualIndent, actualContent) =
1374→                                if (it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1375→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(it.text.length)
1376→                                } else if (it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() && it.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine()) {
1377→                                    it.getFirstElementOnSameLine().text.splitIndentAt(correctedExpectedIndent.length)
1378→                                } else {
1379→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(prefixLength)
1380→                                }
1381→                            if (indentConfig.containsUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)) {
1382→                                val offsetFirstWrongIndentChar =
1383→                                    indentConfig.indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)
1384→                                emit(
1385→                                    it.startOffset + offsetFirstWrongIndentChar,
1386→                                    "Unexpected '${indentConfig.unexpectedIndentCharDescription}' character(s) in margin of multiline " +
1387→                                        "string",
1388→                                    true,
1389→                                )
1390→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1391→                                    (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1392→                                        correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1393→                                    )
1394→                                }
1395→                            } else if (actualIndent != correctedExpectedIndent && it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1396→                                // It is a deliberate choice not to fix the indents inside the string literal except the line which only contains
1397→                                // the closing quotes.
1398→                                emit(
1399→                                    it.startOffset,
1400→                                    "Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes",
1401→                                    true,
1402→                                )
1403→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1404→                                    if (it.firstChildNode == null) {
1405→                                        (it as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(
1406→                                            LeafPsiElement(REGULAR_STRING_PART, correctedExpectedIndent),
1407→                                        )
1408→                                    } else {
1409→                                        (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1410→                                            correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1411→                                        )
1412→                                    }
1413→                                }
1414→                            }
1415→                        }
1416→                    }
1417→            }
1418→    }
1419→
1420→    private fun ASTNode.isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression() =
1421→        (prevLeaf()?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevLeaf()?.text == " ") &&
1422→            FUN ==
1423→            prevCodeLeaf()
1424→                .takeIf { it?.elementType == EQ }
1425→                ?.treeParent
1426→                ?.elementType
1427→
1428→    private fun ASTNode.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock() = RETURN_KEYWORD == prevCodeLeaf()?.elementType
1429→
1430→    /**
1431→     * Get the length of the indent which is shared by all lines inside the string template except for the indent of
1432→     * the closing quotes.
1433→     */
1434→    private fun ASTNode.getCommonPrefixLength() =
1435→        children()
1436→            .filterNot { it.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE }
1437→            .filterNot { it.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE }
1438→            .filter { it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" }
1439→            .filterNot { it.text == "\n" }
1440→            .let { indents ->
1441→                val indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote =
1442→                    indents
1443→                        .filterNot { it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() }
1444→                if (indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote.count() > 0) {
1445→                    indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote
1446→                } else {
1447→                    indents
1448→                }
1449→            }
1450→            .map { it.text.indentLength() }
1451→            .minOrNull()
1452→            ?: 0
1453→
1454→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
1455→
1456→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
1457→
1458→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
1459→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
1460→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
1461→
1462→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
1463→        for (child in node.children()) {
1464→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
1465→                val v = child.text
1466→                if (v == "\n") {
1467→                    return true
1468→                }
1469→            }
1470→        }
1471→        return false
1472→    }
1473→
1474→    private fun ASTNode.containsMixedIndentationCharacters(): Boolean {
1475→        assert((this.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression).isMultiLine())
1476→        val nonBlankLines =
1477→            this
1478→                .text
1479→                .split("\n")
1480→                .filterNot { it.startsWith("\"\"\"") }
1481→                .filterNot { it.endsWith("\"\"\"") }
1482→                .filterNot { it.isBlank() }
1483→        val prefixLength = nonBlankLines.minOfOrNull { it.indentLength() } ?: 0
1484→        val distinctIndentCharacters =
1485→            nonBlankLines
1486→                .joinToString(separator = "") {
1487→                    it.splitIndentAt(prefixLength).first
1488→                }
1489→                .toCharArray()
1490→                .distinct()
1491→                .count()
1492→        return distinctIndentCharacters > 1
1493→    }
1494→
1495→    private fun ASTNode.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() =
1496→        elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE || (text.isBlank() && nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE)
1497→
1498→    private fun ASTNode.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() = elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY && text != "\n"
1499→
1500→    private fun ASTNode.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() =

content.text:
I see the issue now. The check `!node.isPartOf(IF)` is too broad - it excludes ALL parenthesized expressions inside IF statements, including those in the condition. But we only want to exclude parenthesized expressions in the THEN/ELSE blocks (the body), not in the condition.

Let me fix this by creating a helper function that specifically checks if the node is in the THEN or ELSE block of an IF:

                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF &&
                (codeStyle != ktlint_official || !node.isPartOf(IF)) ->
                startIndentContext(node)", "new_string": "            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF &&
                (codeStyle != ktlint_official || !node.isPartOfIfBody()) ->
                startIndentContext(node)"}
